text:
Once upon a time, nestled deep within an ancient forest stood a little cottage. Inside lived Oldreamble, an eighty-years-old owl known for telling absurd tales.

One misty morning, Oldreamble decided he wouldn’t tell dreams tonight. Instead, he’d recount a mythological tale.

The story begins with Hyperbole, a lovable giant goose. He wouldn’t just eat an egg. He’d gobble it whole!

One day, Hyperbole went fishing. Hours passed. Fish vanished.

Then Hyperbole took to the skies.

"You’re probably snatched by eagles!" someone cried.

"No!" Hyperbole flapeded.

He flew higher until he saw it.

Hundreds—I counted 'em—stars! Those weren;t eagles!

He grabbed one. Whoosh!

Stars fell.

That night Hyperbole dreamed big.

Tomorrow would rhyme feathers.

He dreamt.

A hare.

Hop hop hop.

Stars fell.

Hyperbole burst.

"Hopping!" he fluttered.

No stars—just him
